text.title: Eloquent Model Conventions
text.summary: Rules for $fillable, $casts, scopes, accessors, soft deletes, and ULID PKs on Laravel 10/11 Eloquent models.

rule.statement: Always declare $fillable on every Eloquent model. Never leave both $fillable and $guarded empty. Use ULID or UUID PKs (use HasUlids) for API-exposed models.
rule.rationale: Empty $fillable + empty $guarded allows mass assignment on every column, enabling IDOR via update endpoints. Auto-increment PKs leak row counts.

rule.statement: Cast every non-string column explicitly: enums to PHP 8.1 backed enum, dates to 'datetime', booleans to 'boolean', JSON to 'array', decimals to 'decimal:N'.
rule.rationale: Without explicit casts, boolean columns return '0'/'1' strings, JSON columns return raw strings, and decimals lose precision in arithmetic.

rule.statement: Use the new Attribute::make(...) accessor/mutator syntax (Laravel 9+). Do not mix with old getXAttribute / setXAttribute methods in the same model.
rule.rationale: Mixing old and new accessor syntax produces undefined behavior when both exist for the same attribute; old-style accessors silently win in some versions.

rule.statement: Define local scopes for filters reused across multiple endpoints (scopeActive, scopeSearch, scopeWithRole). Use Query Objects for multi-condition report filters.
rule.rationale: Scopes are tied to the model and auto-discovered; Query Objects are independent and composable without model coupling.

rule.statement: When using SoftDeletes, explicitly use withTrashed() in any report or admin query that must include deleted records. Never rely on default scopes being excluded.
rule.rationale: The default global scope silently hides soft-deleted rows, causing counts and reports to differ from raw DB state.

rule.statement: Set $with = [] (empty) on all models. Eager load explicitly per query with with(). Never declare $with with relationship names.
rule.rationale: Declaring $with causes hidden global eager loads on every query — even when the relationship is unused — wasting database round trips.

rule.statement: Enable Model::preventLazyLoading(! app()->isProduction()) in AppServiceProvider::boot(). This throws an exception when a lazy relationship is accessed in non-prod.
rule.rationale: Without this guard, N+1 queries are invisible until they appear as production latency. Failing loud in dev surfaces them immediately.
